title: Krylov Complexity in Periodically Driven CFTs and Critical Fermions
abstract: We study Krylov construction in periodically driven conformal field theories and their lattice realisations via critical fermions. Two types of driving are considered: a square-wave drive and a continuous sinusoidal drive. Using the Arnoldi construction, we examine Arnoldi coefficients and return amplitudes in periodically driven conformal field theories in the heating and non-heating phases. In the heating phase, the Arnoldi coefficients approach unity exponentially; in contrast, in the non-heating phase, they exhibit oscillatory behaviour. For the lattice realisations, we further analyse the Krylov complexity of the correlation matrix, quasi energy level statistics, and the graph structure induced by the Floquet operator. Although the two drives exhibit similar Krylov growth on the CFT side, their lattice realisations exhibit markedly different spectral and graph signatures, indicating distinct mechanisms governing the transition between the heating and non-heating phases.
summary: The paper investigates how Krylov complexity and operator growth behave in periodically driven conformal field theories and their lattice fermion counterparts. It identifies distinct signatures in the heating and non-heating phases and demonstrates that the type of driving protocol significantly impacts the spectral and graph-theoretic properties of the system.
main_problem: Characterizing the transition between heating and non-heating dynamical phases in periodically driven (Floquet) systems using Krylov complexity.
main_result: In the heating phase, Arnoldi coefficients approach unity exponentially, while in the non-heating phase, they exhibit oscillatory behavior; additionally, lattice realizations of continuous drives show different spectral and graph signatures compared to square-wave drives.
method: The authors use the Arnoldi and Lanczos algorithms to construct Krylov bases, apply conformal mapping techniques for CFT analysis, and employ graph-theoretic analysis of the Floquet operator's transition matrix.
model_or_system: The study focuses on (1+1)-dimensional Conformal Field Theories (CFTs) and their lattice realizations via 1D critical fermions under two driving protocols: a square-wave drive and a continuous sinusoidal drive.
key_observables: Krylov complexity (Ck), Arnoldi coefficients (h_{n, n-1}), return amplitude/autocorrelation (Gn), level-spacing ratio (r-factor), and Fiedler value.
important_parameters: Driving protocols (square-wave vs. continuous), heating vs. non-heating phases, and the driving strength parameter (delta_f).
main_assumptions: The CFT analysis is restricted to the SL(2, R) sector and (1+1)-dimensional systems.
figures_summary: Figures include schematics of Krylov vector transitions, conformal mapping visualizations, driving protocol diagrams, and plots of Arnoldi coefficients, return probability, and graph connectivity/clustering.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of Floquet dynamics, analyzes the CFT limit using analytical methods, compares this to lattice fermion simulations, explores different driving protocols (square-wave vs. continuous), and concludes with graph-theoretic and spectral analysis of the transition.
why_interesting: This paper is highly relevant for many-body dynamics as it provides a precise diagnostic (Krylov complexity) for distinguishing between thermalizing (heating) and non-thermalizing (non-heating) regimes in driven quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
